title: EIP-7799: System logs
description: Extends eth_getLogs to provide per-block system logs for events not associated with any transaction, such as block rewards (priority fees), withdrawals, and genesis allocations. Introduces a system logs list committed via a new system-logs-root in the execution block header, and updates the JSON-RPC and Engine APIs accordingly.
author: Etan Kissling (@etan-status), Gajinder Singh (@g11tech)
laymanDescription: Adds automatic log entries for ETH balance changes that happen outside of normal transactions, like block rewards and validator withdrawals. This lets wallets see a complete history of all ETH movements using existing log-querying tools, without needing to download every block header.

endUsers.description: Wallets can display a complete ETH balance history including block rewards and withdrawals, improving transparency without requiring user action.

appDevs.description: Applications querying eth_getLogs gain access to system-level events; developers tracking ETH flows no longer need to separately process withdrawals and block rewards.

walletDevs.description: Wallet developers can rely on eth_getLogs alone for complete ETH balance tracking, removing the need to fetch block headers and withdrawal lists.

toolingInfra.description: Indexers, explorers, and analytics tools must handle new system log entries and the systemLogsRoot field in block headers and Engine API payloads.

layer2s.description: No direct impact. Layer 2 bridges or rollups that parse L1 block headers may need to account for the new system-logs-root field.

stakersNodes.description: Validators and node operators benefit from provable priority fee rewards via system logs, simplifying reward accounting.

clClients.description: Consensus clients must extend the ExecutionPayload with a system_logs_root field and validate it during fork-choice processing.

elClients.description: Execution clients must emit system logs for fees, withdrawals, and genesis; compute the system-logs-root; extend the block header and JSON-RPC API.

benefits: Enables complete ETH balance tracking via eth_getLogs without downloading every block header.
Makes priority fee block rewards efficiently provable from the system logs root.
Unifies withdrawal and reward event discovery with the existing log-based querying model.
Reduces complexity for wallets and indexers that currently need multiple data sources for balance history.
tradeoffs: Adds a new field to the execution block header, breaking applications that assume a fixed header layout.
Withdrawal data is duplicated across the withdrawals list and system logs until a future consolidation.

improveUX.description: Wallets gain a single, unified interface (eth_getLogs) for tracking all ETH balance changes, eliminating the need for complex multi-source balance reconstruction.
